topic: Curated factual technical knowledge compiled by the community

entries.q: What is The Book of Secret Knowledge?
entries.a: A collection of inspiring lists, manuals, cheatsheets, blogs, hacks, one-liners, cli/web tools, and more.

entries.q: Who is The Book of Secret Knowledge aimed at?
entries.a: System and Network administrators, DevOps, Pentesters, and Security Researchers.

entries.q: How can I contribute to The Book of Secret Knowledge?
entries.a: By making a pull request with valid and well-reasoned explanations about your changes or comments, following the contributing guidelines.

entries.q: What type of content can be found in The Book of Secret Knowledge?
entries.a: Inspiring lists, manuals, cheatsheets, blogs, hacks, one-liners, cli/web tools, and more.

entries.q: What is the purpose of the Table of Contents in The Book of Secret Knowledge?
entries.a: To provide a clear and easy-to-use index of the main chapters and topics covered.

entries.q: What are some examples of CLI Tools mentioned in The Book of Secret Knowledge?
entries.a: GNU Bash, Zsh, tclsh, bash-it, Oh My ZSH!, and Oh My Fish.

entries.q: What are some examples of Shell plugins mentioned in The Book of Secret Knowledge?
entries.a: z, fzf, zsh-autosuggestions, zsh-syntax-highlighting, and Awesome ZSH Plugins.

entries.q: How can I stay updated with changes and new content in The Book of Secret Knowledge?
entries.a: By subscribing to the RSS/Atom feed of commits or checking the repository regularly for new updates.
